construct: Demonstrates spatial reasoning, logical, critical and analytical thinking in solving real life problems.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Interprets and computes relationships of numbers within and across different number systems

subSkills.label: Manipulate numbers using different properties to solve real life problems; creates patterns and puzzles of different types of numbers

elementsOfConstruct.description: Models real life situations to solve problems and make informed decisions

subSkills.label: Use algebra to express and solve real life problems
topicAliases: 1Apply functions to model and solve problems
1Apply quadratic functions to model and solve problems

elementsOfConstruct.description: Computes measures and applies spatial relationships to solve real life problems

subSkills.label: Applies measures and geometry in life contexts; derives theorems and formulae; obtains the value of pi practically

elementsOfConstruct.description: Interprets and computes functions to solve real life situations in order to make informed decisions

subSkills.label: Interprets functions, rates of change and summation of quantities in life contexts
topicAliases: Straight lines on the Cartesian Plane

elementsOfConstruct.description: Presents and analyses data to make informed decisions

subSkills.label: Presents and Interprets data in a variety of real-life contexts
topicAliases: Cumulative Frequency and Measures of Dispersion
Statistical Presentations and Measures of central tendency
Statistical Presentations and Measures of Dispersion

components.structureNotes: SBA Form 2: 6 tasks/100. All 5 EoC. 12 tasks across Form 2+3 total.

Section A: 2 compulsory; Section B: 4 items answer 3. Each item 20 marks (answer 5 of 6 = 100). All 5 EoC. Duration 2h30. 70%. (PDF summary table incorrectly shows 200 total marks; body text specifies 100.)

rules: Calculation items: method marks for stages + accuracy mark for correct final answer.
Marks for correct diagrams/graphs and measurement accuracy.
Projects/practicals scored per SBA Guidelines rubrics.
Omission of essential working loses marks.
